title: Isomacrolobium gabunense
taxonomy: Isomacrolobium gabunense is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Isomacrolobium, which is a group of plants native to tropical Africa. The plant is further categorized as a member of the tribe Dialiinae, known for its unique characteristics. Isomacrolobium gabunense is a woody climber, which suggests its classification is closely related to other woody climbers within the family Fabaceae. Further research into its classification may reveal more specific details about its taxonomic position.

The plant's extracts are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help in treating various ailments. intro: Isomacrolobium gabunense, also known as the Gabun tree, is a plant species native to West Africa, belonging to the family Fabaceae. The health benefits of Isomacrolobium gabunense include its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help in treating various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues.
